name: Zara
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Zara's brand language — the Spanish fast-fashion editorial that refuses every e-commerce convention. Canvas is pure white. Type runs an oversized custom serif (Söhne-adjacent or a proprietary modern serif) at hero scale — sometimes 200–400px display headlines that fill the entire viewport. Product photography is editorial fashion-magazine grade: cinematic crops, off-center compositions, models in motion-blurred poses, oversized garments swept by wind. The chrome is so minimal it nearly disappears — no nav bars over imagery, no sticky CTAs, no labels. The site reads as Vogue, not as a department store.

design_language.motion_signature: Cinematic. Hero videos play silent and loop — models walking, garments swirling, slow tracking shots. Display headlines fade in over 1.2s on scroll. Product grids reveal one tile at a time as you scroll, each at 600ms ease-out. The brand voice is 'fashion film', not 'e-commerce app'.

trademark_signals: Display-tier headlines at 200–400px filling the entire viewport — Zara is the brand where the typography itself is the hero
Editorial fashion-magazine photography — off-center crops, motion-blur, oversized garments, wind-swept staging — never product-on-white
Invisible navigation — the nav bar dissolves until hover, models and garments command 100% of the visual space
Zero-radius geometry — every card, button, and tile uses 0px corners — the brand refuses the soft-modern radius convention
Massive negative space — sections are 200–400px tall blocks of pure white between editorial moments — the gap IS the pacing
ai_slop_avoided: Don't add hover lift or shadow on product tiles. Zara refuses every conventional product-card UI affordance — the chrome must stay invisible
Don't reduce display headlines below 80px. The oversized-typography moment IS the brand voice — 48px reads as off-brand
Don't render photography in flat-light catalog style. Zara photography is cinematic — editorial crops, motion, atmosphere
Don't round corners. Zara is a 0px-radius brand; soft rounded geometry breaks the editorial-fashion mood
Don't show price prominently. Price appears at the smallest possible scale — secondary to the image — refusing the convention that price IS the value prop
